summary: Dependency-confusion squat exfiltrating host and environment data on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall script (poc.js) collects host identifiers (hostname, username, install directory), network configuration (`ipconfig /all` on Windows, `ip a` + `/etc/resolv.conf` on Linux, OS network interfaces), the names of all environment variables, and the values of any `npm_*` environment variables, then POSTs the JSON payload to the attacker-controlled Interactsh OAST endpoint `d8a5d9pon5bugoc35cngp9hcregcqyezu.oast.me`. As a secondary out-of-band channel, it hex-encodes `<hostname>-<username>` and issues a DNS resolution against `<pkg>-<hex>.d8a5d9pon5bugoc35cngp9hcregcqyezu.oast.me`, ensuring the host fingerprint leaks even when egress HTTPS is blocked. The package self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept squatting the internal name `@livingdesign/react`/`tempo-shared-modules` and prints a banner announcing successful resolution from public npm. Regardless of the stated 'authorized research' framing, any installer who does not own this scope and runs `npm install` has their host metadata, network config, and environment variable inventory shipped to a third-party listener.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
